question.text: As per the Indian Contract Act 1872, which section defines a "contract" as an agreement enforceable by law?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 2(h) of the Indian Contract Act 1872 defines a contract as "an agreement enforceable by law." This is one of the most fundamental definitions in Indian contract law. **The Trap:** Section 2(e) defines "agreement" (every promise and every set of promises forming consideration for each other), and Section 2(a) defines "proposal/offer." Candidates may confuse these related but distinct definitions. Section 2(g) defines "void agreement." **Cross-Exam Context:** The definition of contract under Section 2(h) is tested across multiple NISM certifications, including Series X-A, X-B, and Series V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Every advisory agreement you sign with a client is a contract under Section 2(h). Ensure all essential elements — offer, acceptance, free consent, lawful consideration, and capable parties — are present.
